comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Validate loot event timestamps before rendering ISO dates.**

Line 55 type-casts parsed JSON to `LootEventResponse` without runtime validation, and Line 110–113 calls `toISOString()` directly. If a JSONL line is parseable but has invalid `ts`, this throws `RangeError` and breaks `status`.

 

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 		for (const line of recent) {
 			try {
-				events.push(JSON.parse(line) as LootEventResponse);
+				const parsed = JSON.parse(line) as Partial<LootEventResponse>;
+				if (
+					typeof parsed?.name === "string" &&
+					typeof parsed?.source === "string" &&
+					typeof parsed?.tier === "string" &&
+					typeof parsed?.ts === "number" &&
+					Number.isFinite(parsed.ts)
+				) {
+					events.push(parsed as LootEventResponse);
+				}
 			} catch {
 				// skip malformed lines
 			}
 		}
```
</details>


Also applies to: 109-113

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@packages/cli/src/status.ts` around lines 53 - 56, The JSONL parse loop
currently blindly casts parsed objects to LootEventResponse (in the block
iterating over recent and pushing into events) and later calls toISOString() on
event.ts (around the rendering that uses toISOString()), which throws for
invalid timestamps; update the parsing code to validate the parsed ts at runtime
before casting—e.g., after JSON.parse check that parsed.ts exists and that new
Date(parsed.ts) yields a valid date (isFinite(date.getTime()))—and only push a
correctly-typed LootEventResponse into events (or otherwise skip/log the
malformed line). Also change the rendering path that calls toISOString() (the
code using event.ts) to defensively create a Date, check validity, and render a
safe fallback (e.g., "invalid date" or omit ISO string) instead of calling
toISOString() unconditionally.
```

</details>

## Walkthrough

This pull request adds a new `status` command to the CLI that reads cached profile, state, and loot data directly from the filesystem without making any network calls. The implementation defines a behavioral specification for the command, creates the core status module with file I/O and formatting logic, integrates it into the CLI router with appropriate help text and dispatch handling, and includes a comprehensive test suite covering edge cases like missing profiles, stale data detection at 24-hour thresholds, and death-related field rendering.

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

**Actionable comments posted: 3**
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
packages/cli/src/status.test.ts (1)
`134-144`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Add a test for invalid `state.json` (not just missing).** The spec/implementation treat malformed `state.json` as “no current activity.” Adding this case protects that behavior from regression.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@packages/cli/src/status.test.ts` around lines 134 - 144, Add a new test mirroring the "missing state.json" case but writing a malformed state.json to ensure invalid JSON is treated as "no current activity": use writeFile(profileCachePath(home), JSON.stringify(profileFixture()), "utf8") to create the profile, then create an invalid state file by writing a non-JSON string into the same cache directory (e.g. path.join(path.dirname(profileCachePath(home)), "state.json")), call runStatus({ home, now: () => NOW }) and assert the same outcomes as the existing test (expect(result.missingProfile).toBe(false); expect(result.output).not.toContain("activity="); expect(result.output).toContain("`@ada`")). ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@packages/cli/src/status.ts`: - Around line 34-41: The reader currently swallows all filesystem errors by returning null/[] in every catch; update readStateJson to only treat ENOENT as recoverable (return null) and rethrow any other errors (e.g., EACCES, ENOTDIR) so callers see real failures; apply the same change to the other cache reader function referenced around lines 61–64 (make it return [] only on ENOENT and rethrow other errors). Ensure you check (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === "ENOENT" and rethrow otherwise in both readStateJson and the other reader. - Around line 53-56: The JSONL parse loop currently blindly casts parsed objects to LootEventResponse (in the block iterating over recent and pushing into events) and later calls toISOString() on event.ts (around the rendering that uses toISOString()), which throws for invalid timestamps; update the parsing code to validate the parsed ts at runtime before casting—e.g., after JSON.parse check that parsed.ts exists and that new Date(parsed.ts) yields a valid date (isFinite(date.getTime()))—and only push a correctly-typed LootEventResponse into events (or otherwise skip/log the malformed line). Also change the rendering path that calls toISOString() (the code using event.ts) to defensively create a Date, check validity, and render a safe fallback (e.g., "invalid date" or omit ISO string) instead of calling toISOString() unconditionally. - Around line 121-125: The current try/catch around readProfileCache(deps.home) swallows all errors and treats them as “no profile”; change it to catch the error into a variable, and only set profile = null for a true "not found" case (e.g. err.code === 'ENOENT' or the library's not-found error), otherwise rethrow or surface the error (log and throw) so permission/path/I/O failures are not misreported—update the block around readProfileCache, profile and deps.home in status.ts to implement this selective error handling. --- Nitpick comments: In `@packages/cli/src/status.test.ts`: - Around line 134-144: Add a new test mirroring the "missing state.json" case but writing a malformed state.json to ensure invalid JSON is treated as "no current activity": use writeFile(profileCachePath(home), JSON.stringify(profileFixture()), "utf8") to create the profile, then create an invalid state file by writing a non-JSON string into the same cache directory (e.g. path.join(path.dirname(profileCachePath(home)), "state.json")), call runStatus({ home, now: () => NOW }) and assert the same outcomes as the existing test (expect(result.missingProfile).toBe(false); expect(result.output).not.toContain("activity="); expect(result.output).toContain("`@ada`")). ```
🪄 Autofix (Beta) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes
